events.text: Patient is a 72-year-old White female with a BMI of 26.0. Medical history includes hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and atrial fibrillation. No known family history of cancer.

At age 70, patient presented with right breast skin thickening and nipple retraction. Biopsy revealed invasive lobular carcinoma (8520/3), right breast NOS (C50.9), grade 2, ER positive (Allred 8/8), PR positive (Allred 7/8), HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (ratio 3.2), Ki-67 25%.

At age 70, right breast skin punch biopsy and core biopsy: invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type, Nottingham grade 2. E-cadherin negative confirming lobular histology. ER positive 95%, PR positive 70%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 3.2). Ki-67 25%.
At age 70, mammogram showed diffuse skin thickening and architectural distortion throughout the right breast, BIRADS 5. MRI confirmed an 8.2 cm area of non-mass enhancement.
At age 70, PET/CT showed right breast diffuse FDG uptake (SUVmax 6.8), right axillary and internal mammary lymph nodes, and peritoneal carcinomatosis with ascites.
At age 70, de novo stage IV (cT4bN2M1) HER2-positive invasive lobular carcinoma with peritoneal metastases. Unusual HER2 amplification in lobular histology.
At age 70, initiated first-line THP (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) with close cardiac monitoring given cardiac comorbidities. Cardiology clearance obtained.
At age 70, baseline LVEF 50% on echocardiogram. Borderline low. Cardio-oncology consultation obtained. Continued HER2-directed therapy with close surveillance.
At age 70, after cycle 1 of THP, developed grade 3 diarrhea requiring IV hydration. Pertuzumab dose reduced for subsequent cycles.
At age 71, grade 2 nausea and grade 2 fatigue on THP. Grade 1 peripheral edema. LVEF monitored every 3 cycles.
At age 71, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response with decreased peritoneal disease burden and resolution of ascites. Breast mass decreased.
At age 71, docetaxel held after 4 cycles due to grade 2 peripheral neuropathy and cumulative fatigue. Continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab plus letrozole.
At age 71, LVEF 45% on echocardiogram. Trastuzumab held. Optimized heart failure medications (lisinopril uptitrated, carvedilol added). Repeat echo in 4 weeks showed LVEF 50%. Resumed trastuzumab.
At age 72, PET/CT showed progressive peritoneal disease despite maintenance therapy. New peritoneal nodules identified.
At age 72, transitioned to second-line T-DM1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.

At age 72, patient with progressive metastatic lobular carcinoma. Discussed T-DM1 and future options including T-DXd. ECOG 1. Palliative care co-management initiated for symptom control. Goals of care discussion documented.
At age 72, grade 1 thrombocytopenia on T-DM1. LFTs stable. Cardiac function monitored closely given prior LVEF decline.
At age 70, genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx): CDH1 frameshift mutation (consistent with lobular histology), ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA H1047L, ESR1 wild-type. TMB 3 mutations/Mb. MSS.
At age 72, CT abdomen after 3 cycles of T-DM1 showed stable peritoneal disease. No new sites of metastasis. Ascites has not recurred.
At age 72, echocardiogram on T-DM1 showed LVEF 48%. Cardio-oncology recommends continuing with close monitoring. BNP mildly elevated at 180 pg/mL. No overt heart failure symptoms.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 70, right breast skin punch biopsy and core biopsy: invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type, Nottingham grade 2. E-cadherin negative confirming lobular histology. ER positive 95%, PR positive 70%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 3.2). Ki-67 25%.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 70, mammogram showed diffuse skin thickening and architectural distortion throughout the right breast, BIRADS 5. MRI confirmed an 8.2 cm area of non-mass enhancement.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 70, PET/CT showed right breast diffuse FDG uptake (SUVmax 6.8), right axillary and internal mammary lymph nodes, and peritoneal carcinomatosis with ascites.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 71, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response with decreased peritoneal disease burden and resolution of ascites. Breast mass decreased.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 72, PET/CT showed progressive peritoneal disease despite maintenance therapy. New peritoneal nodules identified.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 72, patient with progressive metastatic lobular carcinoma. Discussed T-DM1 and future options including T-DXd. ECOG 1. Palliative care co-management initiated for symptom control. Goals of care discussion documented.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 70, genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx): CDH1 frameshift mutation (consistent with lobular histology), ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA H1047L, ESR1 wild-type. TMB 3 mutations/Mb. MSS.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 72, CT abdomen after 3 cycles of T-DM1 showed stable peritoneal disease. No new sites of metastasis. Ascites has not recurred.

Please correlate clinically.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
